text.heading: XXX UNIVERSITY
OFFICE OF THE REGISTRAR
text.p: We are writing to acknowledge and appreciate your continued interest in the academic development of our young University. I am therefore, please to inform you of Management's approval of your appointment as Senior Lecturer in the Adjunct Lectureship cadre of the University in the Department of English Language & Literary Studies, School of Human Resource Development of our College of Development Studies during the 2009/2010 session.
Your responsibilities and our expectations in this position shall include the following:

i) Prompt and quality delivery of teaching services at whatever level of assignment. We expect that an Associate Lecturer should put in a minimum of 8 contact hours with students per month. We are glad about your commitment to both the undergraduate and postgraduate courses in your particular case.
ii) Monitoring and supervision of assigned students for seminar presentations.
iii) Quality involvement in presentation and evaluation of students' seminar papers.
iv) Quality assistance to students in understanding research focus and topic selection.
v) Quality supervision to assigned student(s) in all respect of timely completion of their programme(s).
vi) Filing of periodic reports to both Postgraduate School and the College on issue of performance by assigned student(s) with a new to assisting them to achieve excellence through timely mentoring.
text.heading: Terms of Appointment:
text.p: (a) Relationship:The Associate lectureship appointment is an agreement with you to provide specified academic services to indicated University students at determined location, time and in the required quality.
You will be paid N per annum which will be made available either quarterly or at end of session as lump sum. Remuneration may not be paid in advance.

(i) Your transport allowance is dependent on distance between your location of tenure appointment and the University.Lecturers are requested to arrange consolidated teaching periods to minimize frequency of their visit. Transport allowance will be paid as approved by the University or at current rate subsisting in the University. A lecturer can visit only for the maximum number of times that his/her travel costs can be factored into the maximum travel expenditure budgeted.
(ii) Comfortable accommodation shall be provided during your stay on campus for this purpose.
(iii) Feeding arrangement or allowance in lieu at N 1,000 per day shall be paid for every full day of specific associate lectureship responsibility.

You are expected to pass on your agreed timetable, after your interaction with the students, to the Dean of your College and copy the Postgraduate School once agreed. An advance notice of postponement, where unavoidable, should be communicated to the students through the Postgraduate School.

We are very well pleased to have you as part of the educational revolution at University and are therefore looking forward to receiving you. We see you fulfilled in this honoured involvement in human development.
